candidate_problems.text: How can 'capacity to generate, preserve, and improve error-correcting explanations under novel criticism' be operationalized without collapsing back into proxy output metrics? The conjecture shifts the target from answers to feedback architecture, but it leaves open what concrete, falsifiable indicators distinguish genuine explanatory error-correction from merely better performance, rhetoric, or benchmark gaming.

What principled criterion separates 'error-correcting explanations' from adaptive patchwork or regularity-fitting in complex systems? The conjecture relies on a strong explanation/fit distinction, but it remains unresolved how to identify when revisions constitute deeper unification rather than local repairs, especially in systems whose internal representations are opaque or distributed.

Is epistemic progress fundamentally system-relative or can it be compared across different inquiry architectures and social scales? The conjecture treats progress as an emergent property of interaction structure, raising the open question of whether there are invariant measures that allow comparison between individuals, labs, institutions, and AI-human hybrids, or whether progress is only intelligible relative to a system’s own criticism-and-memory loops.
